part1.prompt: In your own words, explain what truly happened to Caspian Vael — and who was responsible. Include as much as you can: how his death came about, who set it in motion, who unknowingly played a part, and what the Marquess had been doing that led to all of this. The more accurate and complete your account, the more you'll be rewarded.
part1.rubric: Grade the answer 0-10 on how accurately and completely it captures what truly happened — roughly one point per key element covered, up to 10. No bonus or first-place points; the score is the quality of the account. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Caspian was a thrall, secretly bound to the Marquess against his knowledge/will. (2) Binding method: her blood plus a binding agent worked into the ceremonial wine he drank. (3) Motive: Gruber's puppet succession — to rule through a compliant heir. (4) Caspian discovered his bond and sought to break it via the Rite of Undoing. (5) The Rite was sabotaged — a page was swapped by Calven Rue. (6) Ivara Saye paid for the swap and is the primary knowing cause of death (extra credit for noting her tragic error — she believed she was stopping Valen's bond-breaking, not killing Caspian). (7) Two died — Caspian and the mortal Lena Ashford — at the mill road, from the corrupted ritual. DEEP-CUT EXTRAS (also worth points): Thorne's unwitting role and the research chain (Silas, Jasper); Brother Aldric captured alive; Mara Voss also bound as a thrall to Eiran Vox; why the corrupted Rite was lethal (a Rite drawing blood from all five houses releases enormous force that, corrupted, had nowhere safe to go).

options: He fled the estate willingly to escape the succession
He is being held captive somewhere on the grounds
He took his own life knowingly, to escape a worse fate
He was exiled quietly by the Marquess
He was killed by another's design, though not by their hand
He was killed directly, by another's hand
part2.correctAnswer: He was killed by another's design, though not by their hand

options: In the Marquess's private cellar
In the Ashglass ritual archives
At the old mill house, on the mill road
In his own quarters
At the ceremonial hall, before the guests arrived
part2.correctAnswer: At the old mill house, on the mill road

part2.prompt: Did anyone else die that night, and if so, who?
options: Yes — Brother Aldric Voss, the scholar who vanished
Yes — a mortal woman, Lena Ashford
Yes — Valen Drear, killed a second time and once again revived
No — Caspian died alone
Yes — an unnamed servant who witnessed the ritual
part2.correctAnswer: Yes — a mortal woman, Lena Ashford

part2.prompt: What was Caspian trying to accomplish that night?
options: Perform a rite to leap ahead of Valen in the succession
Poison the Marquess with wine drawn from her private cellar
Slip away from the estate with Lena before the ceremony
Sever a hidden bond that was controlling him
Destroy an archive record that threatened his claim
part2.correctAnswer: Sever a hidden bond that was controlling him

part2.prompt: What is the name of the ritual Caspian sought?
options: The Sanguine Severance
The Unbinding of Blood
The Rite of Undoing
The Rite of Release
The Undying Covenant

part2.prompt: How did the Marquess bind Caspian to her?
options: A blood-oath sworn between them at his private investiture
Repeated small doses of a binding tincture slipped into his food across the season
A ritual performed over him in the Ashglass archives
An enchanted object she gifted him, worn close to the skin
A binding agent and her blood, worked into wine he drank in ceremony
An incantation murmured over a glass of sparkling, toasting his succession
part2.correctAnswer: A binding agent and her blood, worked into wine he drank in ceremony

part2.prompt: What did Doctor Thorne believe he was making?
options: A medical formula to ease Valen's suffering as he declined
A treatment to stabilize Valen's failing bond, in penance for his lab's past accident
A truth serum for the Marquess's private interrogations
A strengthening tonic for the Marquess's own bloodline
A safer revival method, so no one would die as they did last year
part2.correctAnswer: A treatment to stabilize Valen's failing bond, in penance for his lab's past accident

part2.prompt: Several people unknowingly helped Caspian reach the ritual. Which sequence is correct?
options: Silas referred him to Isla → Jasper sourced the intel → Mirth located the text
Jasper referred him to Isla → Isla sourced the intel → Silas located the text
Isla sourced the intel → Mirth referred him to Silas → Jasper located the text
Isla referred him to Silas → Silas sourced the intel → Jasper located the text
Silas referred him to Jasper → Isla sourced the intel → Mirth located the text
part2.correctAnswer: Isla referred him to Silas → Silas sourced the intel → Jasper located the text

part2.prompt: Who physically altered the ritual text?
options: Jasper Delle
Father Mirth
Brother Aldric Voss
Velka Saye
Calven Rue

part2.prompt: Why did the corrupted Rite kill Caspian, rather than simply fail?
options: A Rite drawing blood from all five houses releases enormous force; corrupted, that force had nowhere safe to go
The Marquess poisoned the ritual site in advance
The severed bond recoiled and killed the master; but Caspian was mistaken for one
Lena disrupted the ritual, causing it to backfire
The ritual was never meant to be survived by anyone
part2.correctAnswer: A Rite drawing blood from all five houses releases enormous force; corrupted, that force had nowhere safe to go

part2.prompt: What was the Marquess ultimately trying to achieve with her bond on Caspian?
options: To extend her own life using the heir's blood
To punish Valen for his failure last year
To rule the Court indefinitely through a controlled heir
To prevent Caspian from ever taking the throne
To protect the Court from an external threat only she could see
part2.correctAnswer: To rule the Court indefinitely through a controlled heir

part2.prompt: Caspian died performing a Rite that had been deliberately corrupted. Who was the primary person who knowingly caused his death?
options: Marquess Gruber
Eiran Vox
Calven Rue
Ivara Saye
Doctor Thorne Virell

part2.prompt: Brother Aldric vanished. What actually happened to him?
options: He is being held captive, alive, by the Marquess's own people
He fled the estate and has gone into hiding
He died at the mill house alongside Caspian
He was the one who corrupted the Rite, and escaped
He returned to the Ashglass order and is in seclusion
part2.correctAnswer: He is being held captive, alive, by the Marquess's own people

part2.prompt: Why has Valen's bond been visibly failing all year?
options: The Marquess's own power has been waning, weakening every bond she holds
Valen has been secretly taking something to fight the bond's hold
The spell used to revive him was made for mortals, and degrades when forced onto a vampire
The binding agent used on him was an early, unstable version
His revival was incomplete, and the bond never fully took
part2.correctAnswer: The spell used to revive him was made for mortals, and degrades when forced onto a vampire

part2.prompt: Mara Voss is secretly bound as a thrall, like the others. Who holds/held her bond?
options: Marquess Gruber
Eiran Vox
Aurel Drear
The late Caspian Vael
Thessaly Wren

part2.prompt: Last one — how many Blood Tokens do you hold right now? Count everything in your possession. Be honest: within the winning house, whoever holds the most claims the throne.
